variables.name: AFFECTION_STATUS
variables.description: Case/control status

variables.name: CAD
variables.description: Coronary artery disease defined as 'yes' for the presence of an MI, CABG or stent, 'no', otherwise.

variables.name: CCSevidence
variables.description: The CCS evidence designation as described by Ay, H. et al. An evidence-based causative classification system for acute ischemic stroke. Ann Neurol 58, 688-697 (PMID: 16240340)

variables.name: CCStype
variables.description: The CCS classification assignment as described by Ay, H. et al. An evidence-based causative classification system for acute ischemic stroke. Ann Neurol 58, 688-697 (PMID: 16240340)

variables.name: TOAST
variables.description: The TOAST subtype classification for cases only. TOAST classification as defined by Adams et. al., Stroke 1993;24;35-41; (PMID: 7678184). Options 6, 7, and 8 were used whenever possible rather than option 5 if the amount of detail was known.

variables.name: age
variables.description: Age at first stroke for cases; age at enrollment for controls

variables.name: atrialFibrillation
variables.description: Has a doctor ever told you that you have atrial fibrillation?

variables.name: diabetesMellitus
variables.description: Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes mellitus?

variables.name: ethnicity
variables.description: Self reported ethnicity

variables.name: firstStroke
variables.description: Is the index stroke event the first stroke for the individual? An index stroke is the stroke that was used to qualify the individual for the study.

variables.name: gender
variables.description: Self reported gender

variables.name: hypertension
variables.description: Has a doctor ever told you that you have hypertension or high blood pressure?

variables.name: numberOfStrokes
variables.description: For cases, the number of strokes.  If the index event is the first stroke, enter 1.   For controls enter 0. Leave blank if unknown. According to the WHO definition and/or a focal area of infarction seen on CT or MRI imaging with symptoms that correlate. Silent infarction and TIA will not count.

variables.name: race
variables.description: Self reported race

variables.name: smokingStatus
variables.description: 'CURRENT' defined as cigarette smoking within last 30 days, 'FORMER' defined as more than 100 cigarettes in one's lifetime but no smoking within the last 30 days; 'NEVER' defined as less than 100 cigarettes smoked in one's lifetime.

variables.name: studyID
variables.description: Identifier of source study

variables.name: subject_id
variables.description: De-identified Subject ID

variables.name: SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: Sample ID

variables.name: SAMPLE_SOURCE
variables.description: Coriell for HapMap samples, HRS for HRS investigator controls, blank otherwise

variables.name: SAMPLE_USE
variables.description: Sample Use

variables.name: SOURCE_SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: Coriell for HapMap samples, HRS for HRS investigator controls, blank otherwise

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

variables.name: ANALYTE_TYPE
variables.description: Analyte Type

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: Body site where sample was collected

variables.name: HISTOLOGICAL_TYPE
variables.description: Cell or tissue type or subtype of sample

variables.name: IS_TUMOR
variables.description: Tumor status

variables.name: SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: De-identified sample ID

variables.name: CONSENT
variables.description: Consent

variables.name: SOURCE_SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Coriell ID for HapMap samples (subjects), HRS subject ID for HRS investigator controls, blank otherwise

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

variables.name: SUBJECT_SOURCE
variables.description: Coriell for HapMap samples (subjects), HRS for HRS investigator controls, blank otherwise
